description: Cross-protocol transfer: trained on balanced, deployed on natural
clinically_relevant: Tests whether models trained on standardized scans work on natural clinical scans
key_findings: ALL methods degrade severely: 27-72% drop when deployed cross-protocol
PointNet shows best cross-transfer (0.608), likely due to point-independent processing
PointNet++ shows catastrophic cross-transfer failure (0.266, 72% drop)
DINOv3-ft and DGCNN degrade similarly (0.338 vs 0.433)
Training fix (retraining on target protocol) is LARGEST for DINOv3-ft (+0.403)
This confirms: protocol-matched training is essential for all methods
DINOv3-ft benefits most from target-protocol data due to backbone adaptability

ft4_natural_to_balanced.note: Natural→Balanced direction available but within-protocol consistency needs verification
